content.full_text:
November 24, 1976

Mr. Edwin J. Moore  
City Hall, Santa Clara  
1500 Warburton Avenue  
Santa Clara, California 95050

Dear Mr. Moore:

This letter is to confirm the substance of our telephone conversations of November 22 and 23, 1976, concerning the application of Government Code §87100, et seq., to various matters pending before the City Council of the City of Santa Clara.

The Commission has granted a request for an opinion from Mayor Gary Gillmor concerning the legality of his participation in a matter involving an application for a zoning change submitted by the Santa Clara Methodist Retirement Fund, Inc. Pending adoption of that opinion, our interim advice to Mr. Gillmor was that he disqualify himself from participating in decisions concerning the matter in question.

This advice does not, however, extend to Mr. Gillmor's, or any other councilperson's, possible participation in a decision to remand the matter to the Planning Commission in accordance with Section 56-5 of the Santa Clara Zoning Ordinance, provided that such action is taken for the specific purpose of continuing, or holding in abeyance, the matter so that this agency may issue a formal opinion or render informal advice concerning the legality of participation by the Mayor and one or more councilpersons in decisions regarding the application. It is our view that such action would constitute a purely procedural decision, designed to maintain the status quo pending resolution by the FPPC, and that it is within the spirit of the Political Reform Act to permit participation in a decision of this type. Furthermore, based on the facts as developed in our conversations, we are not persuaded that it is reasonably foreseeable that the participation in the decision to remand the matter will "have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally", on any economic interests of the Mayor or the councilpersons in question. Government Code §87103.

Mr. Edwin J. Moore  
November 24, 1976

Thank you for calling upon us for advice. I trust this will resolve your question, at least on an interim basis.

Sincerely,

[handwritten signature] Kenneth W. Goshorn

Kenneth W. Goshorn  
Research Specialist III  
Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:glb

sections.question_synthetic: May the Mayor and City Council members participate in a decision to remand a zoning change application to the Planning Commission when they may have conflicts of interest in the underlying application?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that City Council members may participate in a procedural decision to remand the matter to the Planning Commission to maintain status quo pending formal FPPC opinion, as such action constitutes a purely procedural decision not having a material financial effect distinguishable from effects on the public generally.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal interim advice letter responding to telephonic inquiry; lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers; references pending formal opinion to be issued separately; handwritten signature present; some ambiguity about whether this is fully FPPC's position or interim guidance pending formal opinion

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May the Mayor and City Council members participate in a decision to remand a zoning change application to the Planning Commission when they may have conflicts of interest in the underlying application?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that City Council members may participate in a procedural decision to remand the matter to the Planning Commission to maintain status quo pending formal FPPC opinion, as such action constitutes a purely procedural decision not having a material financial effect distinguishable from effects on the public generally.

embedding.summary: FPPC interim advice letter confirming telephone conversations regarding Government Code §87100 conflicts of interest provisions applicable to Santa Clara City Council decisions on a zoning change application. The Commission advises that while the Mayor should disqualify himself from decisions on the underlying application pending a formal opinion, he and other council members may participate in a procedural decision to remand the matter to the Planning Commission.
